message:
feat(microsoft365): add file operations and calendar CRUD

Implement five new M365 operations:

- m365.files.get_item: retrieve drive item by ID
- m365.files.search: search OneDrive files with query string
- m365.files.create_share_link: generate sharing links with scope/type
- m365.calendar.get_event: fetch single calendar event
- m365.calendar.update_event: patch calendar event fields

Add percent-encoding dependency for URL-safe query params. Full manifest
entries with schemas, network constraints (graph.microsoft.com), and AI
hints. Comprehensive integration tests with mock servers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

+[provides.operations."m365.calendar.get_event"]
+description = "Get a single calendar event by ID"

+type = "object"
+
+[provides.operations."m365.calendar.get_event".network_constraints]
+host_allow = [
+    "graph.microsoft.com",

+]
+port_allow = [443]
+ip_allow = []
+cidr_deny = []
+deny_localhost = true

+deny_tailnet_ranges = true
+require_sni = true
+spki_pins = []
+deny_ip_literals = true
+require_host_canonicalization = true
+dns_max_ips = 16
+max_redirects = 3
+connect_timeout_ms = 10000
+total_timeout_ms = 30000

+[provides.operations."m365.calendar.get_event".ai_hints]
+when_to_use = "Get full details of a specific calendar event by its ID."
+common_mistakes = ["Using a stale event_id after the event has been deleted."]

+related = [
+    "m365.calendar.list_events",
+    "m365.calendar.update_event",
+    "m365.calendar.delete_event",

+
+[provides.operations."m365.calendar.update_event"]

+capability = "m365.calendar.write"
+risk_level = "medium"

+[provides.operations."m365.calendar.update_event".input_schema.properties.end]
+description = "Updated end time object with dateTime and timeZone"
+type = "object"

+description = "Event ID to update"
+type = "string"

+[provides.operations."m365.calendar.update_event".input_schema.properties.location]
+description = "Updated event location"
+type = "object"
+
+[provides.operations."m365.calendar.update_event".input_schema.properties.start]
+description = "Updated start time object with dateTime and timeZone"
+type = "object"
+
+[provides.operations."m365.calendar.update_event".input_schema.properties.subject]
+description = "Updated event subject"
+type = "string"
+
+[provides.operations."m365.calendar.update_event".input_schema.properties.user_id]
+description = "User principal name or 'me'"
+type = "string"
+
+[provides.operations."m365.calendar.update_event".output_schema]
+required = ["event"]
+type = "object"
+
+[provides.operations."m365.calendar.update_event".output_schema.properties.event]
+type = "object"
+
+[provides.operations."m365.calendar.update_event".network_constraints]
+host_allow = [
+    "graph.microsoft.com",
+    "login.microsoftonline.com",
+]
+port_allow = [443]
+ip_allow = []
+cidr_deny = []
+deny_localhost = true
+deny_private_ranges = true
+deny_tailnet_ranges = true
+require_sni = true
+spki_pins = []
+deny_ip_literals = true
+require_host_canonicalization = true
+dns_max_ips = 16
+max_redirects = 3
+connect_timeout_ms = 10000
+total_timeout_ms = 30000
+max_response_bytes = 1048576
+
+[provides.operations."m365.calendar.update_event".ai_hints]
+when_to_use = "Update an existing calendar event's subject, time, attendees, or other properties. Uses PATCH semantics (only changed fields needed)."
+common_mistakes = [
+    "Adding attendees without user confirmation (sends real meeting invites).",
+    "Not specifying timeZone in start/end objects.",
+    "Sending the full event body instead of just the changed fields.",
+]
+examples = ['{"user_id": "me", "event_id": "AAMkAG...", "subject": "Updated Standup", "start": {"dateTime": "2026-03-03T10:00:00", "timeZone": "America/Chicago"}, "end": {"dateTime": "2026-03-03T10:30:00", "timeZone": "America/Chicago"}}']
+related = [
+    "m365.calendar.get_event",
+    "m365.calendar.create_event",
+    "m365.calendar.delete_event",
+]

 [provides.operations."m365.calendar.get_freebusy"]
